LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF UNIGARD INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 2, 2013, TO GIVE ALL PARTIES THE CHANCE TO COMPLY WITH SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B. ALL PARTIES SHALL PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THEIR PAPERS IMMEDIATELY WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-12-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-12-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF UNIGARD INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. UNIGARD'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS, AS A MATTER OF LAW, THAT UNIGARD HAD NO LEGAL DUTY TO DEFEND THOMAS I. PENDERGAST, INC., INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA COAST TRUCK RENTAL ("COAST"), IN THE UNDERLYING WRONGFUL DEATH SUIT (ELAINE IKE, ET AL. V. FORD MOTOR COMPANY, ET AL., CASE NO. CGC-10-497104), PURSUANT TO LABOR CODE 3864. THIS LEGAL FINDING OPERATES RETROACTIVELY. (SEE SCOTTSDALE INS. CO. V. MV TRANSP. 36 CAL.4TH 643, 655.) DEFENDANTS DO NOT DISPUTE THAT UNIGARD RESERVED ITS RIGHTS TO SEEK REIMBURSEMENT FROM COAST, OR ANYONE ELSE, FOR DEFENSE COSTS INCURRED. (SEE JOINT SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS, 13-14, EXH. 2.) CONSEQUENTLY, COAST AND HARCO ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR REIMBURSING UNIGARD FOR THE $323,661.34 IN DEFENSE COSTS INCURRED UNDER EQUITABLE INDEMNITY AND RESTITUTION PRINCIPLES. (SEE SCOTTSDALE, SUPRA; CLARENDON AMERICA INS. CO. V. MT. HAWLEY INS. CO. 588 F.SUPP.2D 1101, 1106 (APPLYING CALIFORNIA LAW.)) UNIGARD IS ALSO ENTITLED PREJUDGMENT INTEREST UNDER CAL. CIV. CODE 3827. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 437C(G). JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HARCO NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS, AS A MATTER OF LAW, THAT UNIGARD INSURANCE COMPANY ("UNIGARD") HAD NO LEGAL DUTY TO DEFEND COAST IN THE UNDERLYING WRONGFUL DEATH SUIT, (ELAINE IKE, ET AL. V. FORD MOTOR COMPANY, ET AL., CASE NO. CGC-10-497104), PURSUANT TO LABOR CODE 3864. THIS LEGAL FINDING OPERATES RETROACTIVELY. (SEE SCOTTSDALE INS. CO. V. MV TRANSP. 36 CAL.4TH 643, 655.) DEFENDANTS DO NOT DISPUTE THAT UNIGARD RESERVED ITS RIGHTS TO SEEK REIMBURSEMENT FROM COAST, OR ANYONE ELSE, FOR DEFENSE COSTS INCURRED. (SEE JOINT SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS, 13-14, EXH. 2.) CONSEQUENTLY, COAST AND HARCO ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR REIMBURSING UNIGARD FOR THE $323,661.34 IN DEFENSE COSTS INCURRED UNDER EQUITABLE INDEMNITY AND RESTITUTION PRINCIPLES. (SEE SCOTTSDALE, SUPRA; CLARENDON AMERICA INS. CO. V. MT. HAWLEY INS. CO. 588 F.SUPP.2D 1101, 1106 (APPLYING CALIFORNIA LAW.)) UNIGARD IS ALSO ENTITLED TO PREJUDGMENT INTEREST UNDER CAL. CIV. CODE 3827. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 437C(G). JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
